title: olmeca recta
intro: Olmeca recta, also known as Mexican prickly poppy, is a flowering plant native to Mexico and Central America. It is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where it is used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant is rich in alkaloids and flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, contributing to its health benefits, such as reducing pain and inflammation, and protecting against oxidative stress. Olmeca recta can be prepared as an infusion, tincture, or salve, and is often used in herbal medicine to promote relaxation and soothe skin irritations.
